headline: Amazon launches 1-hour shipping in US cities to challenge Walmart
summary: Amazon is ramping up its speedy delivery service by offering 1-hour and 3-hour shipping in ‌markets across the U.S., including Los Angeles and Chicago, in ‌its latest efforts to stave off e-commerce competition from its biggest rival Walmart.  Fast delivery has been ​a significant part of Amazon's push to increase basket sizes and shopping frequency.  The company in December announced a separate service called "Amazon Now" to deliver grocery items and everyday essentials in 30 minutes or less in select locations including ‌Seattle and Philadelphia.

content.title: Amazon launches 1-hour shipping in US cities to challenge Walmart

headline: Cash Losing Its Crown in the World’s Biggest Money Corridor
summary: After Maura Fonseca moved to Houston from Mexico in 2011, she often waited hours in line at Western Union to send money to her three children and her mother.  “It’s so much better because it’s cheaper, instantaneous and I don’t have to leave my house,” said the 60-year-old who operates rental properties in Houston and opened a bank account four years ago.  Fonseca is part of a broader transformation in how migrants in the US send money to Latin America.

content.title: Cash Losing Its Crown in the World’s Biggest Money Corridor
content.summary: After Maura Fonseca moved to Houston from Mexico in 2011, she often waited hours in line at Western Union to send money to her three children and her mother.  “It’s so much better because it’s cheaper, instantaneous and I don’t have to leave my house,” said the 60-year-old who operates rental properties in Houston and opened a bank account four years ago.  Fonseca is part of a broader transformation in how migrants in the US send money to Latin America.

headline: SEC may propose shake-up to quarterly earnings reports: WSJ
summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is reportedly preparing a proposal to shift from quarterly to biannual earnings reporting, according to the Wall Street Journal. Yahoo Finance Senior Reporters Brooke DiPalma and Ines Ferré review the details.

content.title: SEC may propose shake-up to quarterly earnings reports: WSJ

headline: General Mills La Tiara Walmart Rollout Tests Growth Story And Valuation
summary: General Mills is relaunching its La Tiara taco shells and seasonings nationally as an exclusive offering at Walmart. The move expands La Tiara from a regional presence to nationwide distribution and is expected to create over 100 new jobs. This represents a large-scale brand rollout and employment expansion for General Mills within its packaged foods portfolio. For investors tracking NYSE:GIS, this La Tiara expansion comes as the stock trades at $38.98, with returns showing a 10.2% decline...
